definition: The gradual gathering or buildup of something over time, such as wealth, knowledge, or snow.
To move upward or climb; to rise to a higher level or position.
Received an advantage or gained something positive from a situation, action, or resource.
The state of being a candidate, especially someone running for a political office or applying for a position.
A group or class of things that share common characteristics; a division used to organize or classify items.
Granted or bestowed something, such as a degree or honor, or held a discussion to exchange ideas.
Postponed or delayed something to a later time, or yielded to someone else's judgment or authority.
The quality of being outstanding or extremely good; a superior level of achievement or performance.
A feeling of intense excitement, happiness, or thrill, often caused by an exciting experience.
To attract and hold someone's attention or interest intensely; to captivate or charm someone.
The second month of the year, having 28 days in a common year and 29 days in a leap year.
Resembling or having the qualities of fire; intensely hot, passionate, or bright.
The system or group of people that officially controls and makes decisions for a country, state, or community.
Something that makes it difficult to accomplish a task or make progress; an obstacle or impediment.
Having the quality of being funny, amusing, or comical.
The practice of claiming to have moral standards or beliefs to which one's own behavior does not conform; pretending to be something you are not.
Free from outside control or influence; capable of thinking or acting for oneself without relying on others.
Relating to the ability to think, reason, and understand complex ideas, or a person who engages in serious study and thought.
Too attractive, appealing, or powerful to be resisted or refused.
The state of feeling sad or unhappy because of a lack of companionship or social connection.
A skillful or carefully planned movement or action, often used to describe steering a vehicle or a strategic action to gain an advantage.
To make or produce something, typically on a large scale using machinery; also refers to the process of making goods from raw materials.
The legally or formally recognized union of two people as partners in a relationship, or the ceremony in which this union takes place.
Something that is much smaller than the usual size; a tiny, scaled-down version of something larger.
Difficult to understand or explain; something that is strange, puzzling, or shrouded in secrecy.
An event or incident that takes place; the fact or frequency of something happening.
Left out or excluded, either intentionally or by mistake; failed to include something.
A personal belief, judgment, or viewpoint about something that is not necessarily based on proven facts.
The loss of the ability to move part or all of the body, often due to nerve damage; it can also refer to a state of complete inability to act or function.
Relating to a specific, individual person or thing rather than a general one; also means especially great or notable, as in giving particular attention to detail.
The quality of continuing to work toward a goal despite difficulties, obstacles, or discouragement.
The people employed by an organization, business, or military service; staff or workforce.
Feeling or causing a sudden, overwhelming sense of fear or anxiety that may lead to irrational behavior.
Stimulated or aroused (as in curiosity or interest), or alternatively, irritated or resentful.
The state of having, owning, or controlling something; an item that someone owns.
The state or condition of something being able to happen or exist; a potential outcome or option.
The quality of being useful, realistic, and suited to actual conditions rather than being purely theoretical.
The repeated exercise of an activity to improve a skill, or the actual application of an idea or method.
The condition of being considered more important than something else; priority in rank or order.
Liked or wanted more than another option; chosen as the more desirable alternative.
An unfair opinion or judgment formed about a person or group without sufficient knowledge or reason.
The process of getting ready for something or making something ready for use.
Widespread or commonly occurring in a particular area or at a particular time.
The head of a school, or an adjective meaning first in importance or rank.
A special right, advantage, or benefit granted to a particular person or group.
Almost certainly; used to express that something is very likely to be true or to happen.
An established series of steps or actions followed in a particular order to accomplish a task.
To begin or continue doing something, or to move forward in a particular direction.
A teacher of the highest rank at a college or university who typically specializes in a particular academic subject.
Well-known and important, or standing out so as to be easily noticed.
The way in which a word or language is spoken, including the sounds and stress patterns used.
To follow or chase someone or something, or to continue working toward a goal.
An amount or number of something, often one that can be measured or counted.
Short tests or assessments, often given in school to check understanding of a topic.
To move back or withdraw gradually, such as floodwaters pulling back or a hairline moving backward.
To be given, presented with, or paid something; to accept or take in something offered or sent.
A suggestion or piece of advice about the best course of action, or a formal statement praising someone's qualities or abilities.
A mention or citation of a source of information, or a person who can speak to your character or qualifications.
A large unit of soldiers in an army, typically commanded by a colonel, or a strict, organized system of doing things.
The act of doing or saying something again, often multiple times, in order to practice or emphasize it.
A business establishment where meals are prepared and served to customers.
A strong, regular, repeated pattern of movement, sound, or beats, especially in music or poetry.
Extremely silly, absurd, or unreasonable; deserving to be laughed at.
The act of giving up something valuable or important for the sake of something or someone else.
Showing great disrespect or irreverence toward something considered holy or sacred.
A fixed amount of money paid to an employee on a regular basis, typically monthly or biweekly, in exchange for their work.
A plan that lists the times at which events, tasks, or activities are intended to take place.
To grab hold of something suddenly and forcibly, or to take possession of something by authority or force.
A faculty by which the body perceives the world (such as sight or hearing), or a feeling that something is the case; also refers to good judgment or reasoning.
To divide or move apart from one another; as an adjective, it means existing independently or not joined together.
A non-commissioned military or police rank, typically above a corporal, responsible for supervising lower-ranking personnel.
To a very serious or harsh degree; extremely or strictly.
Giving off or reflecting bright light; also used figuratively to mean outstanding or excellent.
Resembling something or someone else without being exactly the same; having characteristics in common.
In a genuine, honest, and heartfelt manner; commonly used as a closing in formal letters.
A student in the second year of high school or college.
In a precise, exact, or clearly defined manner; used to single out something particular.
An individual example or sample of something, often used in science to refer to a sample taken for examination or study.
Not moving; fixed in one place or position. (Not to be confused with 'stationery,' which refers to writing materials.)
A carved, molded, or cast figure of a person, animal, or object, typically made of stone, metal, or wood and often displayed as a work of art or memorial.
To achieve a desired goal or outcome, or to come after something or someone in order or position.
A specific method or way of doing something, especially one that requires skill or practice.
Describing someone whose mood changes quickly and unpredictably, or something that is unreliable in how it functions.
A natural inclination or likelihood to behave in a particular way or for something to happen.
Done with great care and attention to every detail, leaving nothing incomplete or overlooked.
An extremely sad or devastating event, or a serious dramatic work that ends with the downfall of the main character.
The act of moving someone or something from one place, person, or situation to another.
Cruel and oppressive rule or government by a single leader or small group who holds absolute power.
Describing a decision or opinion that is fully agreed upon by everyone in a group, with no one disagreeing.
Without any doubt; used to emphasize that something is certainly true or will definitely happen.
Not needed or more than what is required; excessive or redundant.
Up to the point in time or the event mentioned; before a specified time.
Under normal conditions; most of the time or in most cases.
A space entirely empty of matter, or a device that uses suction to clean surfaces by drawing up dust and debris.
A character in a story or real life who is evil, cruel, or commits crimes; the antagonist or 'bad guy.'
The state of the atmosphere at a particular place and time, including temperature, precipitation, wind, and cloudiness.
In a strange, unusual, or unexpected manner.
To destroy or severely damage something, or the remains of something that has been destroyed, such as a crashed vehicle or ship.
Twisting or squirming the body, usually as a result of intense pain or discomfort.
